english: Thgy.; W.: *da lam tar lo* (da lam tar lo) they say the road is open now; also with a definitive subject: *'a-čé'kŭ-lig toṅ' lo* ('a lce sku lig stong lo) the mistress asks for the key; *ko kóm-se rag lo* (kho skom se rag lo) he says he is thirsty (yet also in these cases a speaking on hearsay may be meant: somebody tells me that Mrs. N.N. asks for etc.); *tsór-lo* (tshor lo) report, rumour W., also *tóṅ-lo* (stong lo) and *lób (?)-lo* (lob lo) are said to have a similar signification; *śé-lo* (shes lo) and *ríg-lo* (rig lo) W. are expressions of which I cannot give a satisfactory explanation; bsád-lo byas kyaṅ krám-pa yin (bsad lo byas kyang khram pa yin) Mil., prob.: though he may get a name (in the world) by his learned discussions, he is after all a liar. — lo (lo) 3 prob. occurs only in col. language and more recent pop. literature; Dzl. ༢vV, 17 lo (lo) is a corrupt reading for ysol (gsol). — 4. num.: 146. Comp. lo-skor (lo skor) (Cs. also lo-kor (lo 'khor)) cycle of years, a period of twelve years; it is the usual manner of determining the exact time of an event, which also tolerably well suffices for the short space of a man's life. If for instance a person in a dog-year (e. g. 1874) says that he is a byí-lo-pa (byi lo pa), it may be guessed by his appearance, whether he is 10 or 22, 34, 46 etc. years old, and thus also in other cases accidental circumstances must help to determine the precise date of an event. Occasionally, however, the cycles are counted, e. g. lo-skór brgyad (lo skor brgyad) 96 years Glr. Besides this cycle of 12 years there exists another of 60 years which is formed (in imitation of Chinese chronology) by combining those 12 names of animals with the names of the (so called) five elements, śiṅ (shing) wood, me (me) fire, sa (sa) earth, lčags (lcags) iron, ču (chu) water. Each of these elements is named twice, followed, the first time by po (pho), and the second by mo (mo); when signs of gender may also be omitted without altering any thing in the matter. Thus śiṅ(-po)-kyi-lo (shing pho kyi lo), śiṅ(-mo)-pag-lo (shing mo phag lo), me(-po)-byí-lo (me pho byi lo), me(-mo)-glaṅ-lo (me mo glang lo) are our years 1834, 35, 36, 37, and 1894, 95, 96, 97 etc. — lo-krims (lo khrims) (v. lo-tóg-gi krims (lo tog gi khrims)) ceremonies, at the beginning of harvest. — lo-gráṅs (lo grangs) prop. date (of the year), Sch. also: lo-gráṅs tsáṅ-ma (lo grangs tshang ma) being of (full) age. — lo-mgó (lo mgo) Cs. the beginning of a year, new-year's day. — lo-rgyús (lo rgyus) v. rgyus (rgyus). — lo-ṅán (lo ngan) a bad year, a poor harvest. — lo-čág (lo chag) Cs. 'every second year'. — lo-čúṅ (lo chung) or nyúṅ (nyung) C. young, lo-nyuṅ-nyúṅ (lo nyung nyung) very young. — lo-nyés (lo nyes) = lo-ṅán (lo ngan). — lo-snyíṅ (lo snying) Sch. 'year, period or stage of life' (?). — lo-tóg (lo tog) or -tóg (-tog) the produce of the year, harvest, crop, lo-tóg rṅá-ba (lo tog rnga ba) to reap it, to gather it in. — lo-tó (lo tho) almanac. — lo-dod (lo 'dod) Mil. earthly-minded, sinner? — lo-dpyá (lo dpya) annual tribute. — *lo-pí* (lo phyi) = srus-púd (srus phud), C. — lo-p'yág (lo phyag) (Ld. *lob-čag* (lob chag)) embassy sent every year to the king to renew the oath of allegiance. — lo-p'yéd (lo phyed) half a year. — lo-tsán (lo tshan) annual produce, harvest, lo-tsan čé-ba (lo tshan che ba) a rich, abundant harvest Glr. — lo-légs (lo legs) v. légs-pa (legs pa). — lo-bśál (lo bshal) = lo-tó (lo tho) Cs. — lo-yséb (lo gseb) Sch. a stack, a heap of corn (?).
Ssk. world, lo-ke-śwa-ra (lo ke shwa ra) = अवलोकितेश्वर.
, or lo-tóg (lo tog), v. lo (lo), compounds.

, W. *lób-ma* (lob ma), leaf, ló-ma lhuṅ (lo ma lhung), brul (brul) B., C., *lób-ma ḍil (lob ma dril) or ḍul soṅ* (drul song) W. the leaves have fallen; *lób-ma ṭá-mo* (lob ma thra mo) an acerose or pine-leaf; lo-dáb (lo 'dab) = ló-ma (lo ma).

(v. Ssk. लोच् to speak?) the (art of) translating, sgra daṅ ló-tsa slób-pa (sgra dang lo tsa slob pa) to learn the language and the (art of) translating Glr.; also ló-tsa sgyúr-ba (lo tsa sgyur ba) to translate Pth.; ló-tsa-ba (lo tsa ba) translator (of Buddhist works) lo-čéṅ (lo chen) great translator, seems to be a certain title; lo-páṇ (lo pan) for ló-tsa-ba daṅ páṇḍi-ta (lo tsa ba dang paN+Di ta).

seems to be nearly the same as lóg-pa (log pa) adj, te-tsom lóg-ge-bai ṅáṅ-la (the tshom log ge ba'i ngang la) prob.: entertaining irrational doubts or scruples; baṅ-rím lóg-ge-ba (bang rim log ge ba) an inverted baṅ-rím (bang rim) q. v.; lóg-ge-ba-la kyer (log ge ba la khyer) he took it back again Mil.

I. vb., pf. and secondary form of ldóg-pa (ldog pa), q. v., 1. to return, to go back, yúl-du (yul du) Glr.; *nam 'óa-te ča dug* (nam 'o the cha dug) W., *nam lóg-ne dó-gyu yin* (nam log nas do rgyu yin) C. when will you
